rulings.Case Title: JAMES D. BANKS VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 24, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF JAMES BANKS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the Second Affirmative Defense for Right to Assert Additional Defenses is sustained without leave to amend. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that "Right to Assert Additional Defenses" is an affirmative defense. To the extent Defendant appears to be reserving the right to amend answer at a later date, this is also not an affirmative defense. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES D. BANKS VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 24, 2021 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT JAMES BANKS' DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. The cause of action for services rendered, as stated in opposition is based on the theory of quantum meruit. Such cause of action requires factual support that "the services were rendered under some understanding or expectation of both parties that compensation therefor was to be made." Cross-complaint currently appears to allege that in exchange for exclusive possession of the premises Banks requested and Courthouse Ventures provided services for which reasonable rental value is being sought. No other services are alleged. In the context of the quantum meruit claim this appears to be an allegation that Banks had exclusive possession and it was the understanding of both parties that compensation would be rendered in exchange. Is Court House Ventures alleging some kind of a rental agreement between the parties? Courthouse Ventures shall have a leave to amend to allege what services were rendered to Banks and what was the understanding/expectation of both parties regarding compensation. If Courthouse Ventures is in fact alleging some form of implied rental agreement (combination of current allegations in the complaint and reference to quantum meruit theory in opposition at 3-17) Courthouse Ventures shall so allege. Courthouse Ventures shall also only allege a claim for damages for reasonable rental value that is not already made in the pending unlawful detainer in which reasonable rental value is sought for a period from February 27, 2017 to entry of judgment. The cause of action for quiet title appears to be incomplete. Paragraph 18 stops in mid-sentence suggesting that Courthouse Ventures intended to include the date of determination of quiet title. Courthouse Ventures shall have leave to clarify. Furthermore, Courthouse Ventures claims to be an equitable owner of the property and asserts that Banks claims and interest adverse to Courthouse Ventures. The Court of Appeals in its September 11, 2020 Order in this action stated: "A quiet title cause of action generally has two elements: (1) "the plaintiff is the owner and in possession of the land," and (2) "the defendant claims an interest therein adverse to [the plaintiff]." Courthouse Ventures shall have leave to amend to allege facts to support both elements or to allege facts supporting any exception to the general rule. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY SERVICES VS. CRYSTAL LEI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 24, 2021 line 4. PLAINTIFF LEGAL RECOVERY SERVICES' MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION is DENIED. No new facts, law or circumstances that could not have been presented at the time of the hearing on the demurrer appear in the motion. Plaintiff was provided multiple opportunities to allege facts supporting non-time barred fraudulent/voidable/improper transfers of Tony Fu's assets to the defendants. Plaintiff failed to do so and provides no explanation for such failure, instead re-arguing the demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
